symbols: Christ in dazzling white — the unveiled divine glory of the Son of God
Moses appearing in clouds — the Law bearing witness to Christ
Elijah appearing in clouds — the Prophets bearing witness to Christ
three disciples fallen down — human inability to bear the full weight of divine glory
mountaintop — the place of divine revelation, echoing Sinai and Horeb
bright cloud — the Shekinah glory of God's presence as at the tabernacle
description: Christ stands at the center of a hilltop, dressed in radiant white garments, arms slightly extended. Above him in clouds, two figures appear — one at the left (Moses) and one at the right (Elijah), each seated in glory. Below, three disciples are fallen to the ground in various postures of awe and terror — one shields his eyes, another falls prostrate, the third looks up in astonishment. A landscape with rolling hills stretches behind. The sky is filled with dramatic clouds and divine light emanating from Christ.
theological_description: Gerard David's Transfiguration depicts one of the most theologically significant moments in the Gospels. Matthew records: 'Jesus took with him Peter and James, and John his brother, and led them up a high mountain by themselves. And he was transfigured before them, and his face shone like the sun, and his clothes became white as light. And behold, there appeared to them Moses and Elijah, talking with him' (Matthew 17:1-3). Peter offered to build three tabernacles, 'when a bright cloud overshadowed them, and a voice from the cloud said, This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased; listen to him' (Matthew 17:5). The Transfiguration reveals Christ's true divine nature — the glory he had with the Father before the world existed (John 17:5). Moses represents the Law and Elijah the Prophets — together they confirm that all of Scripture points to Christ. Luke adds the crucial detail that Moses and Elijah 'spoke of his departure, which he was about to accomplish at Jerusalem' (Luke 9:31), connecting the glory of Tabor to the suffering of Calvary. Peter later testified as an eyewitness: 'We were eyewitnesses of his majesty. For when he received honor and glory from God the Father... we ourselves heard this very voice borne from heaven, for we were with him on the holy mountain' (2 Peter 1:16-18).

scripture_refs.reason: The Transfiguration narrative — Christ's glory revealed, Moses and Elijah appearing, the Father's voice
Moses and Elijah speaking of Christ's departure to be accomplished in Jerusalem
Peter's eyewitness testimony of Christ's majesty on the holy mountain
Christ's prayer to be glorified with the glory he had before the world existed — momentarily revealed here
Moses's face shining after meeting God on Sinai — Christ's radiance surpasses Moses on this new mountain
